I’m an avid Agatha Christie fan and was excited that they were doing another movie.  I don’t generally watch movie trailers because they tend to reveal too much of the storyline.  I went to watch this movie on opening night, I was very disappointed that Tina Fey was in this movie.  She’s not a good actress and she was miscast in this film.  It’s a shame, because it took away from the movie experience.  The rest of the cast were wonderful.
Just got out, loved this movie. Actually really well done

❤❤❤❤ Just saw the film it was absolutely great I loved it every single bit of it. Tina Faye doesn't awesome job in her character in a serious role. The only criticism that I would have is the fact that when they did the filming of it the shooting of it is very dark the scenes are sometimes hard to see because it is so dark.  The only other comment I have is I love Agatha Christie and at the end of the credits it says that this was based on Halloween party and it absolutely was not based on Halloween Party.  But it is not at all.  This is AI got the Christie tell story of its own kind and I really loved it and I would highly recommend everyone go see it who loves agatha Christie and spooky vibes and just an excellent scary tail that will keep you guessing until the end. Although I figured it out very early on.❤❤❤❤

Saw it yesterday and it’s super good! Very different from the book (like it’s not even the same story, he just used some of the names) but it was really clever and well performed and all the scares were really cool.
So the plot is different from the novels??

@sugiartokahar938 entirely. Instead of London we’re in Venice (duh) and instead of Joyce being a little girl claiming she witnessed a murder she’s now a grown woman who’s a medium (Michelle Yeoh) and it’s a whole new story.

The thing that puts me off immediatly is that if anybody done any research they,d know Agathas image of Poirot IS David Suchet & Albert Finney so Ken has quite clearly  gone for a see see me moustache to let you know ,whoever  out hams him, whose film it is.

Well, I just saw this movie yesterday. I didn't have a chance to write down my reviews. So I'm doing it now while I have some time. I was about 30 of us to go and see this movie. And a theater of 60 five, so probably about a 1/2 wanted to go see this movie. My review was I enjoyed it very much as a matter of fact, I have to applaud that they got me. They set the mystery up so well that I could not figure out who the killer was and usually I can figure out who these killers are within 30 minutes of a movie by a stumped me on this one. They threw me for a loop and I didn't see it so congratulations. I hope there's another one to follow this movie. My review is I give it a 13 out of 13 skulls and say definitely check this one out.
Looks like Poirot has met his match Folks
